com.newland.nsdk.core.api.internal.crypto

Interface Crypto

  • All Superinterfaces:
    Module

    public interface Crypto
extends Module
    Provides the ability to encrypt and decrypt data with keys in the PIN pad.

    How to get this module:

         Crypto crypto = (Crypto)NSDKModuleManagerImpl.getInstance().getModule(ModuleType.CRYPTO);

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      CipherOutput decrypt(SymmetricKey key, CipherType cipherType, PaddingMode paddingMode, byte[] iv, byte[] data)
      Decrypts data with the key which is already exist in the PIN pad.
      byte[] decryptAsym(AsymmetricKey key, AsymAlgorithmParameters algorithmParameters, byte[] data)
      Decrypts data with the asymmetric key which is already exist in the PIN pad.
      CipherOutput encrypt(SymmetricKey key, CipherType cipherType, PaddingMode paddingMode, byte[] iv, byte[] data)
      Encrypts data with the key which is already exist in the PIN pad.
      byte[] encryptAsym(AsymmetricKey key, AsymAlgorithmParameters algorithmParameters, byte[] data)
      Encrypts data with the asymmetric key which is already exist in the PIN pad.
      byte[] generateCSR(CSRFileType fileType)
      Generates CSR file data.
      MACOutput generateMAC(byte keyId, MACType macType, byte[] iv, byte[] data)
      Generates MAC for a block of data with the specified key.
      MACOutput generateMAC(DUKPTDerivateKey key, MACType macType, byte[] iv, byte[] data)
      Generates MAC for a block of data with derived key by AES DUKPT key.
      byte[] getRandom(int len)
      Gets random data.
      void initCSR(CSRParameters parameters)
      Initials CSR handle and sets the basic information.
      void releaseCSR()
      Releases CSR handle.
      byte[] signAsym(AsymmetricKey key, AsymAlgorithmParameters algorithmParameters, byte[] hash)
      Gets signature data.
      void verifyAsym(AsymmetricKey key, AsymAlgorithmParameters algorithmParameters, byte[] hash, byte[] signedData)
      Verifies if the signature is correct.

    • Method Detail

      • generateMAC

        MACOutput generateMAC(byte keyId,
                      MACType macType,
                      byte[] iv,
                      byte[] data)
               throws NSDKException
        Generates MAC for a block of data with the specified key.

        Example:

             byte keyId = 2;
     MACType macType = MACType.TDES_X99;
     byte[] data = ISOUtils.hex2byte("88888881113366677883373215563223abcdeffdaabbccdd");

     try {
         MACOutput macOutput =  crypto.generateMAC(keyId, macType, null, data);
         if(macOutput!=null){
             // Handle the MAC result: macOutput.getData()
         }
     } catch(NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the exception
     }
        Parameters:
        keyId - [Required] The key used to generate MAC. This key shall already be generated by KeyManager before.
        macType - [Required] MAC type, see MACType
        iv - [Optional] Initial Vector. This is required for CBC mode. 8 bytes for TDES, 16 bytes for AES.
        data - [Required] The data used to generate MAC.
        Returns:
        MAC output. See MACOutput.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • generateMAC

        MACOutput generateMAC(DUKPTDerivateKey key,
                      MACType macType,
                      byte[] iv,
                      byte[] data)
               throws NSDKException
        Generates MAC for a block of data with derived key by AES DUKPT key.

        Example:

             DUKPTDerivateKey key = new DUKPTDerivateKey();
     key.setKeyID((byte) 2);
     key.setDerivateKeyLen(16);
     key.setDerivateKeyType(KeyType.AES);
     key.setDerivateUsage(DUKPTDerivateUsage.MAC);

     byte[] data = ISOUtils.hex2byte("88888881113366677883373215563223abcdeffdaabbccdd");
     try {
         MACOutput macOutput = crypto.generateMAC(key, MACType.AES_DUKPT_LAST, null, data);
         // Handle the MAC result: macOutput.getData()
     } catch (NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the exception
     }
        Parameters:
        key - Derived key by AES DUKPT key. The following parameters required:
        • Key.keyID: AES DUKPT key ID
        • DUKPTDerivateKey.derivateKeyType: Derived key type, see KeyType
        • DUKPTDerivateKey.derivateUsage: Derived key usage, DUKPTDerivateUsage.MAC_GEN, DUKPTDerivateUsage.MAC_VERIFY, DUKPTDerivateUsage.MAC_BOTH
        • DUKPTDerivateKey.derivateKeyLen: Derived key len. It shall be less than AES DUKPT key len. 16/24 for derived DES key. 16/24/32 for derived AES key.
        macType - [Required] MAC type, see MACType. For MACType.AES_DUKPT_X919 and MACType.AES_DUKPT_UNIONPAY_ECB, only DES key is allowed to derive.
        iv - [Optional] Initial Vector. This is required for CBC mode. 8 bytes for TDES, 16 bytes for AES.
        data - [Required] The data used to generate MAC.
        Returns:
        MAC output. See MACOutput.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • encrypt

        CipherOutput encrypt(SymmetricKey key,
                     CipherType cipherType,
                     PaddingMode paddingMode,
                     byte[] iv,
                     byte[] data)
              throws NSDKException
        Encrypts data with the key which is already exist in the PIN pad.

        Example 1:

             SymmetricKey key = new SymmetricKey();
     key.setKeyID((byte) 2);
     key.setKeyUsage(KeyUsage.DATA);

     byte[] data = ISOUtils.hex2byte("88888881113366677883373215563223abcdeffdaabbccdd");

     try {
         CipherOutput cipherOutput =  crypto.encrypt(key, CipherType.DES_ECB, PaddingMode.NONE, null, data);
         // Handle the encrypted data: cipherOutput.getData()
     } catch(NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the exception
     }

        Example 2: Using derived DUKPT key

             DUKPTDerivateKey desKey = new DUKPTDerivateKey();
     desKey.setKeyID((byte) 2);
     desKey.setKeyUsage(KeyUsage.DUKPT);
     desKey.setDerivateUsage(DUKPTDerivateUsage.DATA_ENC);
     desKey.setDerivateKeyType(KeyType.AES);
     desKey.setDerivateKeyLen(16);

     byte[] data = ISOUtils.hex2byte("88888881113366677883373215563223abcdeffdaabbccdd8888888111336667");
     try {
         CipherOutput cipherOutput = crypto.encrypt(desKey, CipherType.AES_DUKPT_ECB, PaddingMode.NONE, null, data);
         // Handle the encrypted data: cipherOutput.getData()
     } catch (NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the exception
     }
        Parameters:
        key - [Required] The key used to encrypt data. see SymmetricKey. This key shall already be generated by KeyManager.generateKey(com.newland.nsdk.core.api.common.keymanager.KeyGenerateMethod, com.newland.nsdk.core.api.common.keymanager.SymmetricKey, com.newland.nsdk.core.api.common.keymanager.Key) before.
        • Key ID is required.
        • Default key usage: KeyUsage.DATA. If using derived DUKPT key, default key usage is KeyUsage.DUKPT
        • If using derived DUKPT key, the following required:
          • Derivate key type: see KeyType
          • Derivate key usage: DUKPTDerivateUsage.DATA_ENC, DUKPTDerivateUsage.DATA_DEC, DUKPTDerivateUsage.DATA_BOTH
          • Derivate key len: 16/24 for derived DES key, 16/24/32 for derived AES key. It shall be less than the len of AES DUKPT key.
        cipherType - [Required] Cipher type. See CipherType
        paddingMode - [Optional] Padding mode, see PaddingMode, default value is PaddingMode.NONE if this is set to null.
        iv - [Optional] Initial vector required for CBC encryption. 8 bytes for TDES, 16 bytes for AES.
        data - [Required] The data to be encrypted. Data length shall be multiple of 16 for AES. Data maximum length shall not be more than 2000.
        Returns:
        Encryption result. See CipherOutput
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • decrypt

        CipherOutput decrypt(SymmetricKey key,
                     CipherType cipherType,
                     PaddingMode paddingMode,
                     byte[] iv,
                     byte[] data)
              throws NSDKException
        Decrypts data with the key which is already exist in the PIN pad.

        Example 1:

             SymmetricKey key = new SymmetricKey();
     key.setKeyID((byte) 2);
     key.setKeyUsage(KeyUsage.DATA);

     byte[] data = ISOUtils.hex2byte("DD55F3341D5ECFF5FBC3336594620743D316FC7C3EABE42E");

     try {
         CipherOutput cipherOutput =  crypto.decrypt(key, CipherType.DES_ECB, PaddingMode.NONE, null, data);
         // Handle the decrypted data: cipherOutput.getData()
     } catch(NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the exception
     }

        Example 2: Using derived DUKPT key

             DUKPTDerivateKey desKey = new DUKPTDerivateKey();
     desKey.setKeyID((byte) 2);
     desKey.setKeyUsage(KeyUsage.DUKPT);
     desKey.setDerivateUsage(DUKPTDerivateUsage.DATA_DEC);
     desKey.setDerivateKeyType(KeyType.AES);
     desKey.setDerivateKeyLen(16);

     byte[] data = ISOUtils.hex2byte("DD55F3341D5ECFF5FBC3336594620743D316FC7C3EABE42EDD55F3341D5ECFF5");
     try {
         CipherOutput cipherOutput = crypto.decrypt(desKey, CipherType.AES_DUKPT_ECB, null, null, data);
         // Handle the encrypted data: cipherOutput.getData()
     } catch (NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the exception
     }
        Parameters:
        key - [Required] The key used to decrypt data, see SymmetricKey. This key shall be generated by KeyManager.generateKey(com.newland.nsdk.core.api.common.keymanager.KeyGenerateMethod, com.newland.nsdk.core.api.common.keymanager.SymmetricKey, com.newland.nsdk.core.api.common.keymanager.Key) before.
        • Key ID is required.
        • Default key usage: KeyUsage.DATA. If using derived DUKPT key, default key usage is KeyUsage.DUKPT
        • If using derived DUKPT key, the following required:
          • Derivate key type: see KeyType
          • Derivate key usage: DUKPTDerivateUsage.DATA_ENC, DUKPTDerivateUsage.DATA_DEC, DUKPTDerivateUsage.DATA_BOTH
          • Derivate key len: 16/24 for derived DES key, 16/24/32 for derived AES key. It shall be less than the len of AES DUKPT key.
        cipherType - [Required] Cipher type. See CipherType.
        paddingMode - [Optional] Padding mode, see PaddingMode, default value is PaddingMode.NONE if this is set to null.
        iv - [Optional] Initial vector required for CBC decryption. 8 bytes for TDES, 16 bytes for AES.
        data - [Required] Data to be decrypted. Data length shall be multiple of 16 for AES.
        Returns:
        Decryption result. See CipherOutput
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • encryptAsym

        byte[] encryptAsym(AsymmetricKey key,
                   AsymAlgorithmParameters algorithmParameters,
                   byte[] data)
            throws NSDKException
        Encrypts data with the asymmetric key which is already exist in the PIN pad.

        Example:

             AsymmetricKey key = new AsymmetricKey();
     key.setKeyID((byte) 3);
     key.setKeyUsage(AsymKeyUsage.DATA);
     key.setKeyType(AsymKeyType.RSA);

     AsymAlgorithmParameters parameters = new AsymAlgorithmParameters();
     parameters.setMessageDigestType(MessageDigestType.SHA256);
     parameters.setEncodingMode(AsymEncodingMode.PKCS_V21);
     parameters.setCryptoMode(AsymCryptoMode.PUBLIC);

     byte[] data = ISOUtils.hex2byte("0102030405060708090A0B0C0D0E0F");

     try {
         byte[] result = crypto.encryptAsym(key, parameters, data);
     } catch (NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the error
     }
        Parameters:
        key - [Required] The key used to encrypt data, see AsymmetricKey. This key shall be generated by KeyManager.generateKey(com.newland.nsdk.core.api.common.keymanager.KeyGenerateMethod, com.newland.nsdk.core.api.common.keymanager.SymmetricKey, com.newland.nsdk.core.api.common.keymanager.Key) before.
        • [Required] Key ID
        • [Required] Key usage
        • [Required] Key type
        algorithmParameters - [Required] Algorithm parameters, see AsymAlgorithmParameters.
        • [Optional] Message digest type is required when encoding mode is not AsymEncodingMode.PKCS_V15.
        • [Required] Encoding mode
        • [Required] Crypto mode
        data - [Required] Data to be encrypted.
        Returns:
        Encryption result.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • decryptAsym

        byte[] decryptAsym(AsymmetricKey key,
                   AsymAlgorithmParameters algorithmParameters,
                   byte[] data)
            throws NSDKException
        Decrypts data with the asymmetric key which is already exist in the PIN pad.

        Example:

             AsymmetricKey key = new AsymmetricKey();
     key.setKeyID((byte) 3);
     key.setKeyUsage(AsymKeyUsage.DATA);
     key.setKeyType(AsymKeyType.RSA);

     AsymAlgorithmParameters parameters = new AsymAlgorithmParameters();
     parameters.setMessageDigestType(MessageDigestType.SHA256);
     parameters.setEncodingMode(AsymEncodingMode.PKCS_V21);
     parameters.setCryptoMode(AsymCryptoMode.PUBLIC);

     try {
         byte[] decryptedData = crypto.decryptAsym(key, parameters, encryptedData);
     } catch (NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the error.
     }
        Parameters:
        key - [Required] The key used to decrypt data, see AsymmetricKey. This key shall be generated by KeyManager.generateKey(com.newland.nsdk.core.api.common.keymanager.KeyGenerateMethod, com.newland.nsdk.core.api.common.keymanager.SymmetricKey, com.newland.nsdk.core.api.common.keymanager.Key) before.
        • [Required] Key ID
        • [Required] Key usage
        • [Required] Key type
        algorithmParameters - [Required] Algorithm parameters, see AsymAlgorithmParameters.
        • [Optional] Message digest type is required when encoding mode is not AsymEncodingMode.PKCS_V15.
        • [Required] Encoding mode
        • [Required] Crypto mode
        data - [Required] Data to be decrypted.
        Returns:
        Decryption result.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • signAsym

        byte[] signAsym(AsymmetricKey key,
                AsymAlgorithmParameters algorithmParameters,
                byte[] hash)
         throws NSDKException
        Gets signature data.

        Example:

              AsymmetricKey key = new AsymmetricKey();
      key.setKeyID((byte) 10);
      key.setKeyUsage(AsymKeyUsage.AUTH);
      key.setKeyType(AsymKeyType.RSA);

      AsymAlgorithmParameters parameters = new AsymAlgorithmParameters();
      parameters.setMessageDigestType(MessageDigestType.SHA256);
      parameters.setEncodingMode(AsymEncodingMode.PKCS_V15);

      String originalString = "12345678";
      byte[] hash = null;
      byte[] signedData = null;

      try {
          MessageDigest digest = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256");
          hash = digest.digest(originalString.getBytes());
          signedData = crypto.signAsym(key, parameters, hash);
      } catch (NoSuchAlgorithmException e) {
          // Failed to get SHA256 message digest instance.
      } catch (NSDKException e) {
          // Handle the error.
      }
        Parameters:
        key - [Required] The key used to sign data.
        • [Required] Key ID
        • [Required] Key usage
        • [Required] Key type
        algorithmParameters - Algorithm parameters, see AsymAlgorithmParameters.
        • [Required] Message digest type
        • [Required] Encoding mode
        hash - [Required] Hash data to be signed.
        Returns:
        Signed data.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • verifyAsym

        void verifyAsym(AsymmetricKey key,
                AsymAlgorithmParameters algorithmParameters,
                byte[] hash,
                byte[] signedData)
         throws NSDKException
        Verifies if the signature is correct.

        Example:

              AsymmetricKey key = new AsymmetricKey();
      key.setKeyID((byte) 10);
      key.setKeyUsage(AsymKeyUsage.AUTH);
      key.setKeyType(AsymKeyType.RSA);

      AsymAlgorithmParameters parameters = new AsymAlgorithmParameters();
      parameters.setMessageDigestType(MessageDigestType.SHA256);
      parameters.setEncodingMode(AsymEncodingMode.PKCS_V15);

      try {
          crypto.verifyAsym(key, parameters, hash, signedData);
      } catch (NoSuchAlgorithmException e) {
          // Failed to get SHA256 message digest instance.
      } catch (NSDKException e) {
          // Handle the error.
      }
        Parameters:
        key - [Required] The key used to verify signed data, see AsymmetricKey.
        • [Required] Key ID
        • [Required] Key usage
        • [Required] Key type
        algorithmParameters - Algorithm parameters, see AsymAlgorithmParameters.
        • [Required] Message digest type
        • [Required] Encoding mode
        hash - [Required] Hash data.
        signedData - [Required] Signed data to verify.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • getRandom

        byte[] getRandom(int len)
          throws NSDKException
        Gets random data.

        Example:

             // Get random data with length 16.
     try {
         byte[] randomOut = crypto.getRandom(16);
     } catch(NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the exception
     }
        Parameters:
        len - [Required] Length of random data, shall be >0.
        Returns:
        Random data.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • initCSR

        void initCSR(CSRParameters parameters)
      throws NSDKException
        Initials CSR handle and sets the basic information.
        Parameters:
        parameters - [Required] The CSR parameters, see CSRParameters.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • generateCSR

        byte[] generateCSR(CSRFileType fileType)
            throws NSDKException
        Generates CSR file data.
        Parameters:
        fileType - [Required] The CSR file type to be generated. See CSRFileType.
        Returns:
        The according file type CSR data.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • releaseCSR

        void releaseCSR()
         throws NSDKException
        Releases CSR handle.
        Throws:
        NSDKException
